title: The verb být: present tense and negation
summary: Use the present tense of být and its negative forms.
objectives: Conjugate být in the present tense
Choose the form required by a subject

sections.heading: Present tense
sections.body: Být means to be. Czech changes the verb according to person and number. Subject pronouns are often omitted when context is clear.
examples: on, ona, ono je / oni, ony jsou

sections.heading: Examples
sections.body: These forms identify people, occupations, and locations.

sections.heading: Negation
sections.body: Add ne- and write the result as one word. The exception is je, whose negative is není.

resources.title: Original lesson: Conjugation of být
resources.note: Affirmative and negative forms with examples.

exercises.prompt: Type the negative form of je.
